subject: Science

unit.unitOverview: This unit helps young children discover one big, wonderful idea: some things around us are alive, and some things are not alive. A goat is alive. A cooking pot is not alive. A mango tree is alive. A stone is not alive. In this guide you will lead your child, step by step, to look closely at the world Allah made and to notice what makes something a living thing.
learningPath: Preview the goals and core ideas
Learn methods and study worked examples
Complete guided practice, experiments and games
Apply, explain and complete the Unit Challenge

provenance.transformation: Structured directly from the Stage 1 science workbook source documents for screen presentation.

outcomes: Sort things into living and non-living, and say why.
Say that living things move, feed, grow and can have young.
Name some animals and say that they are living.
Name some plants and say that they are living.
Say what living things need to stay alive: food, water and air.
Show care and kindness towards a living animal or plant.

concepts.title: Living Things
concepts.explanation: This unit helps young children discover one big, wonderful idea: some things around us are alive, and some things are not alive. A goat is alive. A cooking pot is not alive. A mango tree is alive. A stone is not alive. In this guide you will lead your child, step by step, to look closely at the world Allah made and to notice what makes something a living thing.
concepts.example: Your child is 5 or 6 years old and is only just learning to read. So you are the reader, the guide, and the play-partner. You will talk, point, sing, and wonder together. Keep every lesson short, warm, and full of…

concepts.title: Non-Living Things
concepts.explanation: Your child is 5 or 6 years old and is only just learning to read. So you are the reader, the guide, and the play-partner. You will talk, point, sing, and wonder together. Keep every lesson short, warm, and full of looking and touching. There is no need to hurry. When your child smiles and asks a question, learning is happening.
concepts.example: This unit works well over about four weeks, doing a little each day. Read the words in bold slowly, and let your child say them back to you.

concepts.title: Animals Are Alive
concepts.explanation: This unit works well over about four weeks, doing a little each day. Read the words in bold slowly, and let your child say them back to you.
concepts.example: You do not need to be a scientist to teach this unit. You only need to be curious with your child. When you do not know an answer, that is wonderful - you can wonder together, and you can ask your AI tutor. The most…

concepts.title: Plants Are Alive
concepts.explanation: You do not need to be a scientist to teach this unit. You only need to be curious with your child. When you do not know an answer, that is wonderful - you can wonder together, and you can ask your AI tutor. The most important thing you can give a young child is your warm attention and plenty of time to look, touch, and talk. Praise their thinking, not just their answers.
concepts.example: Try to teach in short bursts of about ten to fifteen minutes. A small child cannot sit still for long, and that is normal and healthy. Do a little, then play, then come back to it later. Learning through play and real…

concepts.title: What Living Things Need
concepts.explanation: Try to teach in short bursts of about ten to fifteen minutes. A small child cannot sit still for long, and that is normal and healthy. Do a little, then play, then come back to it later. Learning through play and real objects at home is exactly how children of this age learn best.
concepts.example: That animals (like goats, chickens, and cats) are alive, and plants (like mango and banana plants) are alive too.

concepts.title: Caring for Living Things
concepts.explanation: That animals (like goats, chickens, and cats) are alive, and plants (like mango and banana plants) are alive too.
concepts.example: This unit helps young children discover one big, wonderful idea: some things around us are alive, and some things are not alive. A goat is alive. A cooking pot is not alive. A mango tree is alive. A stone is not alive.…

activities.title: Watch a Seed Grow
steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

activities.title: Does a Plant Need Water?
steps: Follow the investigation plan in your experiments book.

activities.title: Sort Living and Non-Living Things
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of plants are alive around your home or outside.
Watch it closely for a few minutes and note three things you notice.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

activities.title: Find the Baby Animals
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of what living things need around your home or outside.
Find examples at home and sort them into groups, then explain your rule.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

activities.title: Draw a Living Thing You Care For
activities.materials: Notebook, pencil and safe things you can find at home
steps: Look for an example of caring for living things around your home or outside.
Change one thing, keep everything else the same, and record what happens.
Write or draw what you observed, using the unit's science words.
Explain your finding to a family member or your teacher.

terms: Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes.
Something that is not alive and never was alive.
A living thing that can move about and eats to live.
A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun.
What living things eat and drink to stay alive.
What living things breathe. We cannot see it, but it is all around us.
Being warm, not too cold. Living things need warmth to stay well.

vocabulary.meaning: Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes.
vocabulary.example: In this guide you will lead your child, step by step, to look closely at the world Allah made and to notice what makes something a living thing.

vocabulary.meaning: Something that is not alive and never was alive.
vocabulary.example: To tell the difference between living things and non-living things.

vocabulary.meaning: A living thing that can move about and eats to live.
vocabulary.example: Can name a baby animal, such as a kid or a chick

vocabulary.meaning: A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun.
vocabulary.example: A small plant in a pot, or a leaf from a mango or banana plant.
That all living things grow, need food, need water, need air to breathe, and need warmth.

vocabulary.meaning: What living things eat and drink to stay alive.
vocabulary.example: [Star] Big Idea for the Whole Unit Living things grow, need food and water, need air, and can make babies.

vocabulary.meaning: What living things breathe. We cannot see it, but it is all around us.
vocabulary.example: That all living things grow, need food, need water, need air to breathe, and need warmth.

vocabulary.meaning: Being warm, not too cold. Living things need warmth to stay well.
vocabulary.example: That all living things grow, need food, need water, need air to breathe, and need warmth.

reasoningPrompts.prompt: Explain the key idea in Plants Are Alive.
reasoningPrompts.modelAnswer: You do not need to be a scientist to teach this unit. You only need to be curious with your child. When you do not know an answer, that is wonderful - you can wonder together, and you can ask your AI tutor. The most important thing you can give a young child is your warm attention and plenty of time to look, touch, and talk. Praise their thinking, not just their answers.

questions.question: Which one is alive?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A camel moves, eats and grows, so it is alive.

questions.question: Which one is NOT alive?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A stone never eats, grows or moves by itself, so it is not alive.

questions.question: What do living things need to stay alive?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: All living things need food and water.

questions.question: A baby goat grows into a...
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Living things grow. A baby goat grows into a big goat.

questions.question: Which of these is a plant?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: A mango tree is a plant. It is living.

questions.question: How do we care for a living plant?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Plants need water and light to stay alive and grow.

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: What living things breathe. We cannot see it, but it is all around us.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Air means What living things breathe. We cannot see it, but it is all around us..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Being warm, not too cold. Living things need warmth to stay well.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Warmth means Being warm, not too cold. Living things need warmth to stay well..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Living / Alive means Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: Something that is not alive and never was alive.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Non-living means Something that is not alive and never was alive..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: A living thing that can move about and eats to live.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Animal means A living thing that can move about and eats to live..

questions.question: Which science word matches this meaning: A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun.?
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Plant means A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun..

rounds.prompt: Which science word matches this meaning: Being warm, not too cold. Living things need warmth to stay well.?

selfAssessment: I can sort things into living and non-living, and say why.
I can say that living things move, feed, grow and can have young.
I can name some animals and say that they are living.
I can name some plants and say that they are living.
I can say what living things need to stay alive: food, water and air.
I can show care and kindness towards a living animal or plant.
